name: Automata Construction (NFA to DFA)
description: A regular language is recognized by a deterministic automaton built in stages: Thompson's construction turns a regex into an NFA, then the subset construction explores sets of NFA states reachable under each symbol to form DFA states; accepting states are marked and the DFA is minimized into an equivalent canonical machine.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Regular language / regex"]
    N2["Build NFA (Thompson)"]
    N3["Epsilon-closure of start"]
    N4["Subset construction"]
    N5{"New DFA state?"}
    N6["Add to worklist"]
    N7["Compute transitions"]
    N8{"All subsets processed?"}
    N9["Mark accepting states"]
    N10["Minimize DFA"]
    N11["Equivalent DFA"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N5 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N4
    N8 -->|yes| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Finite automata and their decision problems

sources.title: Programming techniques: regular expression search algorithm
